name: Incorrect Pointer Scaling
description: In C/C++, pointer arithmetic implicitly scales the operand by the size of the pointed-to data type. When developers manually add byte offsets to typed pointers, the implicit scaling causes access to incorrect memory locations, potentially leading to buffer overflows or information disclosure.
detection_pattern: A TRANSFORM[pointer_arithmetic] node adds a byte-level offset to a typed pointer without accounting for implicit type scaling, and the result is used in a TRANSFORM[dereference] node without a CONTROL[scaling_check] mediator that verifies correct offset computation.
